decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 chat foundations over advanced chart features
decisions.context: Heavy engineering investment in conversation infrastructure (ENG-10722, ENG-10311, ENG-10430, ENG-10385) vs incremental chart improvements (ENG-8544, ENG-10721). Strategy says Nail the Core - need to decide if conversational interface IS the core or distraction from decision capture.
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear definition of 'core' for Explore V2 - is it better charts or AI-assisted exploration?

arguments.for: Conversational AI aligns with 'Decisions Captured' north star - lowers friction to insight. Market expects AI interface. Chat can accelerate decision velocity.
arguments.against: Chart quality affects decision confidence. Broken legends/axes undermine trust. Chat without solid visualization foundation = shaky house. Risk: build chat nobody uses because underlying charts frustrate.

decisions.title: Establish formal data provenance requirements for Canvas
decisions.context: ENG-10503 implementing AnalysisScope for provenance. Canvas's 'Embed Deeper' strategy requires customers trust decisions enough to embed in workflows. Provenance = trust. No clear requirement doc yet.
decisions.blockedBy: Need design review to define what 'good enough' provenance looks like for v1

arguments.for: Provenance is table stakes for enterprise embedding. FedRAMP/compliance needs audit trail. Differentiator vs competitors. Enables 'Build the Moat' - harder to rip out when decision history is embedded.
arguments.against: Slows velocity on Canvas features. Customers haven't explicitly asked. Over-engineering risk. Can add post-MVP if demand materializes.

decisions.title: Commit resources to TRMC IDIQ sole-source strategy or sunset government sales motion
decisions.context: CAGR-24 pursuing test-center funding, CAGR-22 planning conference coverage. Significant BD investment but unclear how gov sales fits 2026 'Embed Deeper' strategy. GovCloud infrastructure work (ENG-10720, ENG-10702, ENG-9694) suggests ongoing commitment, but is it strategic?
decisions.blockedBy: No clear gov market sizing or CAC/LTV analysis vs commercial segments

arguments.for: Gov contracts = large predictable revenue. Sole-source reduces sales cycle. FedRAMP investment already sunk cost. Moat: compliance credentials hard for competitors.
arguments.against: Gov sales cycle conflicts with 'Decisions Per Customer' velocity metric. Custom compliance work diverts eng from product strategy. Margins lower. Embedding risk: gov procurement prevents deep workflow integration.

decisions.title: Define Ask SIFT conversation quality bar before production launch
decisions.context: Multiple conversation features in flight (ENG-10722 rename, ENG-10311 titles, ENG-10603 trace logging, ENG-10385 state machine) but no clear quality gate. Launching bad AI chat experience damages brand worse than delaying.
decisions.blockedBy: No product spec defining minimum acceptable hallucination rate, response time, or accuracy thresholds

arguments.for: Quality bar prevents reputational damage. Forces team to test rigorously. Aligns with Signal>Evidence>Decision - need evidence our AI actually improves decision quality before shipping.
arguments.against: Over-speccing delays learning. Better to ship and iterate with beta users. Market moving fast - risk losing to competitors while we polish.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy context-switching across 30 active eng tickets spanning 4+ product areas. Team fragmented between infrastructure (GovCloud, Edge), polish (chart bugs), and innovation (AI chat). No clear swim lanes.
alerts: 30 active engineering tickets with no clear prioritization signals - risk: team working on everything, shipping nothing
Critical chart bugs (legends, axes, zoom) active for weeks - undermines 'Nail the Core' pillar
6+ conversation-related tickets suggest major feature launch imminent but no quality gate defined
GovCloud work (5+ tickets) consuming resources with unclear strategic ROI
Zero instrumentation work visible - cannot measure North Star metric 'Decisions Captured Per Customer'
